factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across all segments due to increased customer activity and project demand.

evidence.text: Revenues increased due to a $1.73 billion increase in revenues from our Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segment, a $1.13 billion increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and a $737.9 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2022 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating across all segments due to increased customer activity and project demand.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions, driven by customer demand and acquisitions.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 was primarily due to approximately $1.44 billion in revenues attributable to acquired businesses, primarily Blattner, which was acquired in October 2021.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, driven by customer demand and acquisitions.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, driven by increased spending on grid modernization.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 was primarily due to increased spending by our utility customers on grid modernization and hardening, resulting in increased demand for our electric power services.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across all segments, with revenue growth driven by real demand rather than price increases.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2022 was primarily due to increased spending by our utility customers on grid modernization and hardening, resulting in increased demand for our electric power services.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across all segments, driven by real demand rather than price increases.

factors.summary: End markets for Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions are growing fastest, while Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions faces some headwinds from lower emergency restoration services revenues.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 was primarily due to increased customer demand for renewable transmission and interconnection construction services.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End markets for Renewable Energy and Underground Utility are growing fastest, while Electric Power faces some headwinds.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, primarily due to higher compensation expenses and non-cash stock compensation.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily attributable to a $173.0 million increase in expenses associated with acquired businesses, as well as a $26.6 million increase in compensation expense associated with increased salaries, incentive compensation and non-cash stock compensation expense due primarily to increased operational performance and an increase in employees to support the growth of the business.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2022 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to higher compensation expenses and non-cash stock compensation.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are mixed, with gross margins improving but operating margins facing pressure from lower emergency restoration services revenues and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased primarily due to the increase in revenues in all segments. See Segment Results below for additional information and discussion related to segment operating income (loss).
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2022 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are improving, but operating margins face pressure from lower emergency restoration services revenues and supply chain disruptions.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving in the Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment, contributing to higher operating margins.

evidence.text: The increases in operating income and operating margin during the three months ended September 30, 2022 were positively impacted by the increase in revenues, which contributed to higher levels of fixed cost absorption. Also contributing was improved performance across the segment, particularly with respect to our industrial services operations, and the impact of a favorable project close out.
evidence.subsection: Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving in the Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment, contributing to higher operating margins.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment due to lower emergency restoration services revenues and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Operating margin decreased during the nine months ended September 30, 2022 as a result of lower emergency restoration services revenues, which contributed to lower equipment utilization and fixed cost absorption, as well as less favorable results associated with normal variability in overall project timing and project mix, inefficiencies attributable to supply chain disruptions impacting certain operations, and elevated consumable costs.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decreasing capacity utilization due to lower revenues and supply chain issues indicates a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain disruptions, economic uncertainties, and challenges in project timing.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating margin was partially offset by improved performance on various communication projects and a $21.5 million increase in equity in earnings from LUMA and other integral unconsolidated affiliates as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals with risks like supply chain disruptions but also positive project outcomes.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: The effective tax rates for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 and 2021 were 26.3% and 23.2%. The tax rate for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 was unfavorably impacted by the unrealized loss on our investment in Starry described above, for which a valuation allowance was recorded.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2022 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable tax impacts from asset valuation suggest financial strain.

factors.summary: A key catalyst is the favorable close-out of certain projects in the Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segment.

evidence.text: Additionally, there were favorable close-outs of certain projects during the nine months ended September 30, 2021 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable project close-outs indicate positive developments.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand for the company's products, particularly in the Renewable Energy and Underground Utility segments.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 was primarily due to increased customer demand for renewable transmission and interconnection construction services.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing demand from infrastructure spending drives revenue growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing due to higher levels of debt, particularly after the acquisition of Blattner.

evidence.text: Our long-term debt increased significantly at the end of 2021 in connection with our acquisition of Blattner.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2022 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased debt levels post-acquisition pose financial risks.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is creating value, particularly through the integration of Blattner, though it is contributing to higher amortization expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in corporate and non-allocated costs during the nine months ended September 30, 2022 was primarily due to a $225.4 million increase in intangible asset amortization, largely associated with the acquisition of Blattner.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Value creation from acquisitions is offset by higher amortization expenses.
